data.title: Wholesale egg prices drop, but retail rates could take time to catch up
data.description:
Wholesale egg prices have been declining, given the rapid recovery in supply and no significant bird flu outbreaks this month. But it's unclear when retail prices will reflect this decline.

According to data from the U.S. Department of Agriculture, national wholesale egg prices fell to $4.15 per dozen as of March 14, compared to $6.85 a week earlier. That's about half the levels seen at the start of the month.

"However, these declines have yet to be reflected at store shelves and, until they are, demand is expected to remain dampened," the agency said. "Grocers have made progress in maintaining a more consistent stock of shell eggs, but some of this is due to many offering little in the way of price incentives, which helped to reduce or eliminate much of the panic and opportunity buying seen in February."

Last month, consumers on average paid a record $5.90 for a dozen large grade-A eggs, according to the Bureau of Labor Statistics. That's 22% higher than the previous record of $4.82 per dozen in January 2023.

Wholesale prices will likely fall further, given "extreme pressure" from market dynamics, Karyn Rispoli, managing editor at Expana, told CNBC. Expana tracks agricultural commodity prices.

But there's generally a lag of at least 2-3 weeks between a change in wholesale costs and resulting retail pricing. And retailers ultimately choose "how closely they want to track wholesale prices," Rispoli added.

To note, the Department of Justice is investigating the surge in egg prices, and looking into whether large producers have conspired to raise prices or limit supply.

Consumer advocacy group Food & Water Watch has claimed that Cal-Maine Foods (NASDAQ:CALM) raised prices during the bird flu outbreak in 2023 despite its flocks being unaffected. The price hikes allegedly helped the largest U.S. egg producer earn over $1B in "windfall profits" that year.

data.title: KE Holdings Non-GAAP EPADS of $0.16 misses by $0.09, revenue of $4.3B beats by $530M
data.description:
• KE Holdings press release (NYSE:BEKE): Q4 Non-GAAP EPADS of $0.16 misses by $0.09.
  • Revenue of $4.3B (+53.6% Y/Y) beats by $530M.
  • Mobile monthly active users (MAU) averaged 43.2 million in the fourth quarter of 2024, relatively flat compared to 43.2 million in the same period of 2023.

data.title: Shiny at $3,000? SA subscribers called the latest gold rally
data.description:
Just five weeks ago, Seeking Alpha's Wall Street Breakfast ran a poll on whether gold was a buy at current levels as the shiny metal set its sights on $3,000/oz. Bullishness won out in the survey, and bullishness was delivered. Gold (XAUUSD:CUR) closed above the $3K level for the first time on Friday, and continues to extend its historic rally, up another 1% overnight to $3,026/oz.

Note: The WSB polls continue each week, so stay on top of the latest results, like yesterday's question on the future trajectory of Nvidia following the recent stock selloff.

What's driving the gold rally? Only one quarter into the year, the yellow metal is up 15% YTD. It's prompting Wall Street to continue increasing its price targets on bullion, with UBS becoming the latest to raise projections as investors seek safety amid geopolitical and tariff risks. Those aren't the only factors, with global central banks ramping up purchases to diversify their reserves amid concerns over growth, inflation and deficit expansion.

Where to gain exposure? "What about the miners? HUI, the Gold Bugs Index, is +29% since Jan. 1!," Miners to the Moon writes in the latest gold article on Seeking Alpha. "Stick to the underlying!" small supernova responds. "The miners have a) operating costs going up in tandem with the metal, and b) all the operational and political risks; and specific company risk, and c) they've historically been terrible stewards of capital. Just play the metal. Use leverage and/or derivatives on the metal (easy, cheap and liquid) if you want extra juice akin to the miners."

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow futures tread cautiously as interest rate decision nears
data.description:
Stock index futures were muted on Tuesday as the traders awaited the Federal Reserve's rate decision slated for Wednesday.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.2%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.2%, and Dow futures (INDU) -0.1%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was unchanged at 4.32%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 4.05%.

The major averages ended on top on Monday as market players assessed the latest retail sales report and prepared themselves for Wednesday’s FOMC rate decision.

"The rebound from Thursday’s S&P 500 correction continued yesterday, even if the sting in the tail was a fresh decline in U.S. tech mega caps," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

The upbeat tone was helped by the absence of negative trade headlines and was if anything helped by a Bloomberg report that U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer was reinstating a more process-oriented approach ahead of the expected April 2 tariff announcements, Reid added. 

The two-day FOMC meeting will commence today, with the monetary policy decision scheduled to be announced on Wednesday. It is widely expected that the Fed will hold rates steady.

On the economic calendar for Tuesday, the February Housing Starts and Permits and February Import/Export Prices are scheduled.

The Export Price Index is expected to come in at -0.2% M/M, while the Import Price Index is forecasted at -0.1% M/M.

data.title: Commodity Roundup: Oil climbs as Mideast tension back in focus, eyes on Trump-Putin talk
data.description:
Oil prices rose on Tuesday, as tensions in the Middle East have resurfaced, with traders also keeping a close eye on the outcome of talks between Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin.

Nymex crude (CL1:COM) +1.44% to $68.55 a barrel, and Brent crude (CO1:COM) +1.29% to $71.98.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI).

Middle East tensions escalated once again lately with U.S. strikes on the Houthis, while the Houthis retaliated by targeting a U.S. aircraft carrier. 

Unsurprisingly, the Trump administration is taking a more hawkish stance against Iran, having tightened oil sanctions since taking office, ING analysts said.

Trump is meanwhile scheduled to talk to President Putin today about a proposed ceasefire.

“Energy markets will be watching closely for any progress -- particularly whether a potential peace deal might include the resumption of some Russian energy flows. This would be more impactful for natural gas rather than oil, given that the scope to increase natural gas flows is much bigger relative to oil.”

Elsewhere, despite a 1.3% rebound in the Bloomberg Commodity Index, hedge funds continued their third consecutive week of net selling, still reacting to the late-February correction that saw the index drop 4.6% before recovering, Saxo Bank reported.

The total net long across 27 major futures contracts has declined by 44% during this time after hitting a 2-1/2-year high, led by selling across energy and grains.

Energy: The sector’s selloff extended to a seventh week, with the net long position down 62% to 224K contracts during this time—half the three-year average. 

Metals: Gold (XAUUSD:CUR) remained steady before surging to $3,000 per ounce at week’s end, with minimal hedge fund activity seen during the reporting week, Saxo's Head of Commodity Strategy, Ole Hansen noted. 

Silver (+2.4%) and copper (+4.6%) gains contributed to increased net longs in both.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Elbit Systems Non-GAAP EPS of $2.66 beats by $0.71, revenue of $1.93B beats by $150M
data.description:
• Elbit Systems press release (NASDAQ:ESLT): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.66 beats by $0.71.
  • Revenue of $1.93B (+18.4% Y/Y) beats by $150M.

  • Backlog of orders for the year ended December 31, 2024 totaled $22.6 billion, as compared to $17.8 billion as of December 31, 2023.

  • Approximately 65% of the current backlog is attributable to orders from outside of Israel. Approximately 57% of the current backlog is scheduled to be performed during 2025 and 2026.

  • Net cash provided by operating activities in the year ended December 31, 2024 was $534.6 million, as compared to $113.7 million in the year ended December 31, 2023.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Tuesday: GRAB, BIDU, and more
data.description:
Stock futures slipped in premarket trading on Tuesday, while Treasury yields rose as investors braced for the Federal Reserve’s two-day policy meeting. Market participants are closely watching for any signals on future rate cuts amid persistent inflation concerns.

Here are some of Tuesday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Eastman Kodak (NYSE:KODK) +7% – Shares surged after the company reported higher net income and operational EBITDA for Q4, despite a 3% revenue decline to $266M. Gross profit rose 9% Y/Y to $51M, with margin improving to 19% from 17%. GAAP net income soared 420% to $26M, while operational EBITDA jumped 350% to $9M.
  • Grab Holdings (NASDAQ:GRAB) +8% –  Shares climbed after reports that the ride-hailing and food delivery giant has begun due diligence on a potential takeover of Indonesian rival GoTo (OTCPK:GTOFF). Grab is reviewing GoTo’s accounts, contracts, and operations, while both companies' shareholders assess the potential deal's structure and value. Talks remain ongoing, but regulatory concerns could pose hurdles.
  • Baidu (NASDAQ:BIDU) +4% – Shares climbed after the company unveiled two new AI models: Ernie 4.5 and Ernie X1. Baidu made its AI chatbot Ernie Bot publicly accessible ahead of schedule. Ernie X1, a deep-thinking reasoning model, rivals DeepSeek’s R1 at half the price, excelling in Chinese Q&A, writing, and complex calculations. Ernie 4.5, a native multimodal model, offers cost-effective input/output pricing.

Biggest stock losers

  • Bakkt Holdings (BKKT) -31% – Shares plunged after Bank of America (BAC) and Webull Pay opted not to renew their commercial agreements, impacting Bakkt's loyalty and crypto services revenue. BofA accounted for 17% of loyalty services revenue, while Webull represented 74% of crypto services revenue in the nine months ended Sept. 30, 2024.

data.title: Tencent unveils open-source AI models to generate 3D visuals from text
data.description:
Chinese gaming giant Tencent (OTCPK:TCEHY) (OTCPK:TCTZF) has launched new AI services which turn text or images into 3D visuals and graphics, amid a growing AI race in China and globally.

Tencent’s website noted that Hunyuan3D-2.0 is a large-scale 3D asset creation system that generates high-quality textured 3D assets. The generative model consists of two main components — a foundation shape generative model and a large-scale texture synthesis model.

The shape generative model intends to create geometry that aligns with specific conditions, laying a solid foundation for various downstream applications. Meanwhile, the texture synthesis model produces high-resolution and vibrant texture maps and is tolerant to painting either generated or real-world meshes, according to the company's website.

Tencent has released five new 3D-content generators, Bloomberg News reported, citing a company statement.

Tencent did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

The company plans to open-source the model to users. They will help power an upgraded version of Tencent's proprietary 3D engine for games and other content.

Tencent has been making efforts to boost its competitiveness. Last month, the company launched out Hunyuan Turbo S, which is aimed at responding as fast as possible, differentiating itself from the deep reasoning approach of DeepSeek’s (DEEPSEEK) AI chatbot. Tencent had noted that the deployment cost of Hunyuan Turbo S has been greatly reduced.

Tencent is also integrating DeepSeek’s R1 model into its products, such as WeChat search and Yuanbao AI chatbot. 

The measures fit into Tencent’s broader business activities. Gaming studios have tried to find ways to use AI to speed up tasks such as in-game design and pre-production, potentially streamlining the time a game title reaches the market, the report added.

The Chinese AI tech space is heating up with companies launching new AI tools to stay competitive in the rapidly growing AI market.

On Monday, Chinese internet search leader Baidu (BIDU) launched its latest multimodal model foundation model Ernie 4.5 and the deep-thinking reasoning model Ernie X1, which it said delivers performance on par with DeepSeek (DEEPSEEK) R1 at only half the price. Baidu, reportedly, also plans to launch AI model Ernie 5 in the second half of this year.

Earlier this month, local rival Alibaba (BABA) unveiled a new AI model called R1-Omni, which is capable of recognizing human emotions through audio and visual cues. The Chinese e-commerce also released the latest version of its Quark AI assistant mobile application, called New Quark. Last month, Alibaba (BABA) released a new family of AI models called Qwen2.5-VL.

data.title: Trump team weighed sorting trading partners into tiers, simplified plan for reciprocal tariffs - report
data.description:
Trump administration officials have reportedly weighed whether to simplify the task of devising new tariff rates for hundreds of U.S. trading partners by instead sorting nations into one of three tariff tiers.

According to a WSJ report, people close to the policy discussions, emphasized that the situation remains fluid and could evolve in the coming weeks. The Trump administration is still trying to sort how to implement an individualized rate for each nation.

“Many plans have been discussed and when the president is ready to announce a plan the American people will hear from him directly,” White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said.

The simplified 3-tier tariff proposal—with low, medium and high rates—was discussed on Thursday at a meeting featuring the likes of chief of staff Susie Wiles, Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer, deputy chief of staff for policy Stephen Miller, Office of Management and Budget Director Russ Vought, and Senior Counselor for Trade and Manufacturing Peter Navarro, WSJ reported.

Officials also discussed several proposals, including the 3-tier approach. But on Friday, the tiered proposal was ruled out in favor of an individualized approach.

How to determine that tariff rate is the subject of debate and discussion among the team now, the report added.

President Donald Trump said he would be imposing both broad reciprocal tariffs and additional sector-specific tariffs on April 2, with no intention to create exemptions on steel and aluminium tariffs.

President Trump last Thursday threatened  to implement a 200% tariff on imports of all alcoholic beverages from the European Union, in a move that represented another escalation in a simmering trade war between the two parties.

Here are some exchange-traded funds that track the benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500): (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), and (SPXU).
